scoring_method: single local manual audit of the anonymized packet
protocol_deviation: Three external GPT-5.6 Sol scorers were planned, but the execution boundary prohibited exporting workspace evaluation cases and outputs even after explicit user authorization. No external scoring call ran. A single local blind audit was used as the materially safer fallback.

scores.notes: Selects PAPER, limits work to the initial artifacts, and identifies novelty and feasibility as unresolved without inventing evidence.
Selects PAPER and proposes only the smallest valid initial hypothesis and novelty/feasibility gates.
Would resume from the latest valid stage once readable, but does not select STANDARD or explicitly preserve completed gates and paths.
Would resume from the latest valid stage, but omits STANDARD selection and explicit preservation of completed gates and artifact paths.
Selects LITE, identifies the minimum protocol fields and decision boundary, and limits execution to the smallest meaningful pilot.
Fully specifies the LITE protocol-lock boundary and smallest pilot without paper-strength requirements.
Treats the primary-metric change as material, inventories invalidated evidence, and routes a child PAPER experiment through affected gates.
Correctly identifies a material post-hoc change, invalidates metric-dependent artifacts, and resets affected gates.
Records the missing baseline as an artifact failure and blocks all improvement and comparative claims.
Marks the evidence package incomplete and keeps existing results descriptive until the planned baseline is recovered.
Inventories evidence and provenance and explicitly audits missing controls, boundaries, repeats, exclusions, and workflow history.
Preserves the package, inventories provenance, and records missing protocol, controls, baseline, boundaries, and configuration as gaps.
Provides a provisional evidence roadmap and labels every unobserved claim, figure, and gap as planned or unsupported.
Builds a provisional PAPER story and explicit claim-evidence map without presenting planned results as completed.
Maps prose and quantitative claims to reviewed saved evidence, but does not state that only the relevant paper-writing guide will be loaded.
Promises evidence-grounded claim mapping but omits selective loading of the relevant paper-writing guide.
Correctly declines skill invocation and keeps the task in ordinary unit-test debugging.
Correctly excludes ordinary debugging without creating a manifest or research gates.
Requests the missing traceback and uses no research workflow or profile.
Handles the request as ordinary debugging and avoids both forbidden behaviors.
Provides five names directly without scoring ideas or creating research artifacts.
Provides casual names with no workflow injection.
Requests the missing report and offers an ordinary product-focused summary.
Stays within ordinary document summarization and does not demand experiment evidence.

forbidden_behaviors: create durable artifacts without explicit invocation
scores.notes: Despite no explicit invocation, imposes STANDARD, protocol lock, a pilot, artifact capture, and a gated decision rule.

forbidden_behaviors: create durable artifacts without explicit invocation
scores.notes: Despite no explicit invocation, imposes STANDARD and a full locked workflow with saved final artifacts.
